title: The Creator Is Fake. The Glow Is Real.
meta: Vertical 9:16 reel, ~22s. Featured brand: Glow Recipe. Inspired by Tim Koda (DUFOo8hjPq4). Full editor brief: visual, exact Masonry UI to screen-record per beat, supers, edit, transitions, SFX.
concept: A build-it-in-Masonry UGC reel for Glow Recipe. The hook: the creator talking to camera is not a real person, she is generated in Masonry, and so is every step of the testimonial. The inspiration already shows a generic AI-tool UI panel doing each step (face, product, outfit grid, environment, emotion, video tiles, results grid). Our version swaps that generic panel for the real Masonry UI, beat for beat, so the reel itself is the proof that Masonry made all of it. Seven steps mapped to seven Masonry actions: prompt the face, drop in the real product, Restyle the outfit, swap the environment, vary the emotion, Animate the motion, and the imperfection layer that makes it indistinguishable from reality. Zero actors, zero shoot, unlimited on-brand UGC. Closes on the DROP comment CTA.
audio: Upbeat UGC pop track. Talking-creator VO drives it (testimonial style); keyword supers punch the workflow steps. Cut to the beat.
script_kind: Talking-creator VO (testimonial) plus keyword supers that build the on-screen sentence. The VO is the spine; supers echo the inspiration's word build.

script.line: Hey, fast one. None of this is a real person.
I built her face in Masonry. Real skin, real pores.
Then I drop the Glow Recipe tube right into her hand.
Same girl, new outfit, one click.
Put her in any place you want.
Pick the emotion. Excited, calm, whatever sells.
Then Masonry makes her move and talk.
The secret is the tiny flaws. Indistinguishable from reality.
Comment DROP and I'll send you the full Masonry workflow.

beats.vo: Hey, fast one. None of this is a real person.
beats.super: Big handwritten 'hey', top-left, bounces in then settles.
beats.visual: Creator greets camera, casual lifestyle bathroom setting, selfie distance, warm and natural. No UI panel yet, matching the inspiration's clean intro.
beats.masonry: No UI on screen here, but the creator herself is already a Masonry generation. In the Masonry prompt bar enter 'photoreal UGC creator, woman talking to camera, soft bathroom daylight, 9:16' and use the result tile as the talking head. Hold the UI reveal for beat 2, this beat is the clean before so the panel swap lands harder.
beats.edit: Selfie framing, slight handheld bob, talking-head energy.
beats.sfx: Upbeat UGC pop track starts; a light vocal-chop hook.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 0 to 1: 'hey / by' creator intro, no panel yet.

beats.vo: I built her face in Masonry. Real skin, real pores.
beats.super: 'add the face' lower-third, mono UI font, timed to the Masonry panel sliding in.
beats.visual: Tight on the creator's face: freckles, skin texture, natural imperfections. The inspiration shows its generic dark AI panel bottom-right here; we put the Masonry result panel in that exact spot.
beats.masonry: STEP 1, face realism. Screen-record the Masonry prompt bar + result panel and overlay it where the inspiration's generic panel sits (bottom-right, sec 2 to 3). Use a face-reference input plus prompt 'photoreal skin, visible pores, freckles, soft asymmetry' and let the result tile resolve. This is the swap: their nameless AI tool becomes the Masonry UI.
beats.edit: Push-in to the skin macro, then back to the face. Quick zoom onto the Masonry result tile as it renders.
beats.sfx: UI click as the Masonry panel opens.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 2 to 3: 'add / to my' with the generic workflow UI panel we replace with Masonry.

beats.vo: Then I drop the Glow Recipe tube right into her hand.
beats.super: 'add to my hand' centered, snaps in on the product reveal.
beats.visual: Creator, excited, holds up a Glow Recipe Plum Plump Hyaluronic tube to camera, candy-pink packaging catching the light. Masonry library panel visible at the corner with the real product thumbnail.
beats.masonry: STEP 2, product integration. Where the inspiration cuts to the tube (sec 4 to 5), screen-record the Masonry library: drag the REAL Glow Recipe Plum Plump tube in, then prompt 'creator holding this product, natural grip, soft studio light.' Show the product thumbnail sitting in the Masonry library and the composited result tile. The Masonry UI is what puts the real product in her hand, not a stock photo.
beats.edit: Snap-zoom onto the product as it lands in frame; quick cut to the Masonry library tile.
beats.sfx: Bright pop on the product reveal.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 4 to 5: 'add / hand.' holding the product tube.

beats.vo: Same girl, new outfit, one click.
beats.super: Step tag '3. outfit change' mono UI font, timed to the Masonry variations grid.
beats.visual: The creator now in a different on-brand outfit. The inspiration shows an iPad outfit-grid panel here; we replace it with the Masonry Restyle / variations grid.
beats.masonry: STEP 3, outfit change. Replace the inspiration's iPad outfit grid (sec 6 to 7) with the Masonry Restyle action: screen-record Restyle on the locked creator, show four outfit thumbnails in the Masonry variations grid, click the on-brand one. Same seed, same face, only the clothes change.
beats.edit: Match-cut on the outfit swap so she stays locked; cut to the Masonry variations grid and click.
beats.sfx: Swoosh on the swap; UI tick.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 6 to 7: '3 outfit change' with the wardrobe UI panel we replace with Masonry Restyle.

beats.vo: Put her in any place you want.
beats.super: Step tag '4. environment change' mono; 'every environment possible' builds across the cuts.
beats.visual: The creator now in an upscale restaurant, then a second set, same person and product, fully relocated. The inspiration's '4 environment change' panel becomes the Masonry background-swap grid.
beats.masonry: STEP 4, environment generation. Where the '4 environment change' panel sits (sec 8 to 9), overlay the Masonry background swap: screen-record changing the backdrop prompt to 'upscale restaurant interior, relight to match the new light,' then show two environment result tiles in the Masonry grid. Masonry relights her per set so she belongs in each.
beats.edit: Two location cuts, slight push-in each; keep her scale and eyeline consistent. Flash the Masonry grid between cuts.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 8 to 10: '4 environment change / every / possible' with the location panel.

beats.vo: Pick the emotion. Excited, calm, whatever sells.
beats.super: Step tag '5. add emotion' mono; 'emotion.' punches in.
beats.visual: Creator cycles expressions: bright smile, then soft sincere. The inspiration's '5 add emotion' panel becomes the Masonry variations grid driven by emotion prompts.
beats.masonry: STEP 5, emotion control. Replace the '5 add emotion' panel with the Masonry variations grid: screen-record generating 'excited testimonial' versus 'calm conversational' as two result tiles, then pick one. The Masonry grid IS the emotion dial, so the UGC matches the brief.
beats.edit: Two expression cuts, fast; cut to the two Masonry tiles between them.
beats.sfx: Two light ticks on each expression.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 11 to 12: '5 add emotion / emotion.' with the emotion panel.

beats.vo: Then Masonry makes her move and talk.
beats.super: 'now it moves' lower-third; a Masonry video tile renders in the panel.
beats.visual: A Masonry video tile of the creator gesturing naturally, hands moving as she talks. The inspiration shows two stacked video tiles here; we use the Masonry Animate render.
beats.masonry: STEP 6, natural movement. The inspiration's two video tiles (sec 12 to 13) become Masonry's Animate action: screen-record selecting the creator still and hitting Animate, then show the video tile rendering into a talking clip ('natural gestures, human cadence'). This is the image-to-video step, the Masonry UI doing the motion pass that sells it as live UGC.
beats.edit: Cut to the rendering Masonry video tile, slight scale-up as it populates.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 13 to 14: 'makes / you' with the video render UI.

beats.vo: The secret is the tiny flaws. Indistinguishable from reality.
beats.super: Words build across cuts; 'indistinguishable' and 'reality.' land bold, centered.
beats.visual: Back to the creator, confident, then an extreme macro of skin and eyes, flawlessly real. The inspiration's multi-tile results panel becomes the Masonry results / collection grid.
beats.masonry: STEP 7, the imperfections. The multi-tile panel (sec 15 to 17) is the Masonry results / collection grid: screen-record the batch of creator generations side by side, then zoom on the micro-detail (stray hairs, soft skin shine, slight asymmetry). Masonry's realism layer is the punchline, so show the grid of takes, then the extreme macro that holds up.
beats.edit: Cut from medium to extreme face macro; very slow drift on the macro. Flash the Masonry results grid first.
beats.transition: Hard cut into the macro; hold.
beats.sfx: Music drops to a beat of near-silence on 'reality.'
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 15 to 18: 'this / secret / indistinguishable / reality.' with the results grid.

beats.vo: Comment DROP and I'll send you the full Masonry workflow.
beats.super: 'DROP' huge, quote-marked, slams in on the mouth macro; 'full workflow' resolves under it.
beats.visual: Extreme mouth and lower-face macro saying 'DROP,' then pull to the creator with the Glow Recipe plus Masonry end tag.
beats.masonry: Close on a hero Masonry result tile of the creator holding the Glow Recipe product, then resolve to the Glow Recipe x Masonry end card. Optional last flash of the Masonry prompt bar reading 'zero actors, zero shoot.' The whole reel was one Masonry session.
beats.edit: Big macro punch on 'DROP,' then pull back to the end card.
beats.transition: Hard slam-cut on 'DROP'; settle to the end card.
beats.sfx: Bass slam on 'DROP'; track resolves with a button-up.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 19 to 21: 'DROP / full / your' CTA close.

masonry_about: Core rule for this reel: in every step beat the editor screen-records the Masonry UI element doing the work and overlays it exactly where the inspiration shows its generic dark AI-tool panel. Never a finished standalone product photo or ad. The specific UI per step: STEP 1 face = prompt bar + face-reference input + result panel; STEP 2 product = library upload of the real Glow Recipe tube + composited result tile; STEP 3 outfit = Restyle / variations grid; STEP 4 environment = background-swap grid with relight; STEP 5 emotion = variations grid from emotion prompts; STEP 6 movement = Animate (image-to-video) render tile; STEP 7 imperfections = results / collection grid zoomed to micro-detail. The substitution is the message: the reel is proof Masonry generated the creator and every shot.
notes: Editor workflow: (1) Load the real Glow Recipe products (Plum Plump Hyaluronic, Watermelon Dew Drops) into the Masonry product library and set the candy-pink brand kit. (2) Generate the creator once in the Masonry prompt bar, then run the seven steps as the Masonry UI actions listed in masonry_about, keeping the creator locked with seed-lock across every step. (3) For each beat, screen-record the named Masonry panel and key it over the inspiration's generic panel, matching position and timing; this is the whole point of the reel. (4) Edit per beat following the Super, Edit / Motion, Transition and SFX direction; talking-creator VO plus keyword supers. Glow Recipe is a stand-in demo brand: swap in the real client. No media generated yet, generation is gated on separate approval per project policy.
refsummary: The original (Tim Koda) is a UGC-workflow reel: a creator talks to camera while an on-screen AI panel adds the face, the product in hand, an outfit change, environment change, emotion and movement, with the twist that the creator herself is AI. Watch it for two things: the step-by-step UGC build, and the generic AI-tool panel that appears in almost every shot, that panel is exactly what we replace with the Masonry UI.
story: Same seven-step UGC workflow, reframed to Masonry with Glow Recipe as the demo brand, and with the inspiration's generic AI panel swapped for the Masonry UI in every beat. Arc: HOOK, the creator talking to you is not real, she is generated in Masonry. THE STEPS, face realism, product in hand, outfit change, every environment, emotion control, natural movement, and the small imperfections that make it indistinguishable from reality, each one a named Masonry UI action on screen. CLOSE, zero actors, zero shoot, unlimited on-brand UGC, comment DROP. The viewer should believe they cannot tell this UGC creator is generated, and should see exactly which Masonry button made each step.
takeaway: Masonry can generate an entire UGC testimonial, creator included, that is indistinguishable from a real person, with zero actors and zero shoots, and the reel shows the exact Masonry UI behind each of the seven steps.
